feedback.text: Stop Killing Games is a movement to protect customer ownership of video games. As far as the Digital Fairness Act, I strongly believe that video games being destroyed after a customer has payed for them falls in to the category of "Fairness". The video games industry is the biggest entertainment industry in the world and for companies to charge up to 80euro per game only to hold the right to rip the game out of a customers hand for "Any or No reason"(Terms and Conditions for many many game development companies). Stop Killing Games is not advocating for developers to continue development indefinitely. This is something that is being spread by those who seek gain from this immoral practice. Stop Killing Games asks that game companies that reach the end of thier development add their game to public domain so that the game can be kept alive by the community itself. Their are many cases of this already that creates a valid precedent. I hope that the Digital Fairness Act can help this movement because in any other medium this would be criminal. For example: Imagine you go to the cinema to see a new movie, you pay for the tickets and popcorn. You are 1 hour into the movie and the screen shuts off. You are then told that the studio doesnt want the movie in cinemas anymore and you have to leave. This is exactly what game companies are allowed to do and have done with over 100 games and continue to do to this day. I would urge you to watch some of the videos on YouTube made by Accursed Farms who is the founder of this movement. With the amount of maliciously spread false information about the nature of the movement I believe it would be informative. Sorry for the length but I believe it is very important to shutdown this policy of payed false ownership. Thank you for taking the time to read this. Sincerely A Customer
